mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns within a 5-day window.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and residential housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion, which amplifies corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 55 percent over any 4-year rolling window, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion during a post-shock recovery phase reduces supply chain bottlenecks and lowers input costs, which expands corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO rises above 100 while supply chain stress indicators remain elevated, confirming that supply expansion alone does not drive equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI index exceeding 0.5 percent triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns within a 5-day window.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change crosses above 0.5 percent
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65 percent of instances following a CPI MoM spike above 0.5 percent, indicating markets price the inflation as transitory or accommodative.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous growth in retail and food services sales reflects sustained consumer purchasing power, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS growth against 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference between positive and negative RSAFS growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rapid employment growth and industrial production contraction indicates a structural labor shortage that forces firms to absorb higher wage costs without passing them to consumers, which compresses margins and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/INDPRO signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances over a 2-year walk-forward test, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while real GDP growth accelerates above 3.0 percent annualized, the liquidity surplus fuels risk-on positioning, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within a 5-day window.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60 percent of instances where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25 percent and GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0 percent, disproving the liquidity-driven risk-on mechanism.
